description_long: This is a horizontally oriented, cylindrical metal storage tank with rounded ends, supported by two sturdy rectangular legs. The tank's surface is weathered and rust-stained, showing signs of prolonged outdoor exposure, with patches of discoloration and corrosion. On the top, there are two short cylindrical pipe fittings and a large, circular access hatch, which appears to be bolted or latched shut. One end of the tank features a visible warning sign, including a red hazard symbol and a label, indicating the contents may be dangerous or flammable. The tank is constructed primarily from thick steel, designed to withstand internal pressure and environmental wear. Its utilitarian design and rugged build suggest it is intended for industrial or municipal use, likely for storing fuel, chemicals, or water.
description: A weathered, cylindrical steel storage tank with rust patches, pipe fittings, and a large access hatch, supported by two rectangular legs. Warning signage is visible on one end, indicating hazardous contents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full cylindrical body, two pipe fittings, and the large access hatch on top, with both support legs visible.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the circular access hatch, warning signage, and the rounded end of the tank.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the cylindrical body, pipe fittings, and support legs, but with the access hatch partially visible.
description_view_3: Rear end view showing the rounded end of the tank with no visible signage or hatch, and the tops of the pipe fittings.
